channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hunter Biden hires Alex Murdaugh’s lawyer in latest court case; Abbe Lowell out
item.description: South Carolina attorney Richard A. Harpootlian is Hunter Biden's new counsel, as Abbe Lowell has reportedly been dropped from a defamation case being heard in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: In their own words: Trump officials shrugging off Signal leak once decried Clinton's server
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The attempts by President Donald Trump and top leaders of his administration to downplay a security breach that revealed military strike plans in a Signal group chat including a journalist stand in stark contrast to their reaction to Hillary Clinton's use of a home server as secret
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five ways a funding overhaul has transformed Texas community colleges
item.description: More than a year after Texas’ House Bill 8 took effect, some schools have offered free tuition, grown dual credit programs or helped students’ credits transfer when they move on to four-year schools.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Obama vs Trump: Political giants back opposing candidates in widely-watched Wisconsin Supreme Court contest
item.description: Former President Barack Obama and President Donald Trump have lined up on opposite sides of Wisconsin's state Supreme Court contest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Energy and migration will top Rubio's agenda on his Caribbean visit this week
item.description: Weaning Caribbean countries from their dependence on Venezuelan oil and combating illegal immigration will top Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s tour of the Caribbean this week, a visit coming as the Trump administration increasingly focuses its attention on the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump intel officials testify on threat from drug cartels as Dems press them on leak of attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's top intelligence officials stressed to Congress the threat they said was posed by international criminal gangs, drug cartels and human smuggling, testifying in a hearing Tuesday that unfolded against the backdrop of a security breach involving the mistake
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RNC asks states for details about their voter files, part of a larger effort to question elections
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Republican National Committee on Tuesday launched a massive effort to probe voter registration lists nationwide amid a broader strategy to seize on voter rolls to question the integrity of elections.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kristi Noem says US has almost total control of border
item.description: High-level cartel associates and gang members are still trying to slip through — but law enforcement is intercepting them.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Intelligence leaders: We didn’t share classified information in Signal chat group
item.description: Some of the nation’s top intelligence officials will appear before Congress in a pair of hearings this week. Two were participants in a widely criticized war plans group chat on Signal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends Waltz after Signal breach
item.description: President Trump said he still has confidence in national security adviser Mike Waltz in an interview with NBC on Tuesday, following a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was included in a text message chain with officials about plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois mandates halal, kosher meal options in state facilities
item.description: The law mandates the availability of halal and kosher meal options in state-run schools and facilities upon request.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Letter carriers speak out against Trump plans to privatize U.S. Postal Service
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul and Duluth on Sunday against calls to privatize the U.S. Postal Service. U.S. Sen. Tina Smith called the move a “hostile takeover” by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: The Pentagon's DEI purge: Officials describe a scramble to remove and then restore online content
item.description: The Pentagon has faced questions from lawmakers, local leaders and citizens over the removal of military heroes and historic mentions from Defense Department websites and social media pages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP educator-leaders respond to Trump‘s dismantling of the Education Department
item.description: Assistant Majority Leader and Chair of the Senate Education Finance Committee Sen. Mary Kunesh, DFL-New Brighton — who is a library media specialist — and House Education Policy Committee Co-Vice Chair Rep.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News
